name: Portable Toilet Cabin
description:
A portable toilet cabin solves one specific problem: a site or venue that needs a solid, lockable, full-structure toilet — not a flimsy cubicle that tips or cracks under daily crew use. It is a standalone sanitation product, manufactured and delivered ready to connect.

What a Portable Toilet Cabin Is — The Full Enclosed Unit

A portable toilet cabin is a full enclosed, free-standing sanitation unit — a complete steel cabin structure with its own walls, roof, floor, lockable door, and toilet fittings. Unlike a lightweight cubicle, it is the sturdiest fixed-position unit in a portable toilet range, built to stay on one site through a long project rather than being shifted daily.

White portable toilet cabins arranged at an outdoor event — clean units suited to public-facing venues.

This page covers the core fixed-position unit in detail. It is one product within SAMAN's wider portable toilet range, where you can compare unit counts, water-supply options, and material choices across every format.

How the Cabin Is Built — Frame, Panels, Floor, and Door

The cabin is built around a mild-steel structural frame, not a moulded shell. That is what gives it the rigidity to take repeated daily use from a full crew. The frame carries insulated wall panels, a single-slope steel roof that sheds rain cleanly, and a reinforced anti-skid floor that stays stable on a levelled site. The hinged door is lockable, which matters on open construction sites and public event grounds.

A portable toilet cabin set up as a fixed sanitation point near a factory gate.

Component Specification
Standard size 8 × 8 × 7 ft (configurable)
Structural frame MS box-section steel frame
Wall panels Insulated steel wall panels (configurable)
Roof Single-slope steel roof sheet
Flooring Reinforced anti-skid floor
Door Lockable hinged door
Toilet fitting Western or Indian pan (specify on order)
Ventilation Wall vent + roof vent provision
Water inlet ½-inch inlet; overhead tank or septic-tank option
Exterior finish Powder-coated frame, panel colour options

The 8 × 8 ft footprint is larger than a standard FRP cubicle, so the same shell can be supplied as a single roomy unit or split into a two-cubicle layout. An MS portable toilet cabin built this way is heavier than a moulded FRP toilet, which is the trade-off for a unit that survives several project deployments instead of one season.

Inside the Cabin — Pan, Ventilation, and Water Provision

Inside, the unit is fitted with your choice of a western or Indian-style pan, a water inlet, and cross-ventilation through a wall vent and a roof vent so the cabin does not become stale in summer heat. For sites without a mains line, the cabin can be supplied with an overhead tank or as a portable toilet cabin with a septic tank built in, so waste is held and treated on site. The full comparison of mains, tank, and bio-digester setups is laid out on the range page; on this unit you simply specify which provision you want fitted before dispatch.

How Many People One Cabin Handles a Day

A portable toilet cabin serving a construction crew, placed within easy reach of the work area.

A single-pan portable toilet cabin comfortably serves a working crew of about 25 people across a full shift — the same ratio Indian construction-site sanitation rules are built around. Where one cabin is enough, this unit is the right buy; where you are sizing a fleet for a large workforce or a big event, the range page explains how many units a given headcount needs. For a small site office, a remote field team, or a single event point, one full cabin unit usually does the job on its own.

Price and What's Included

A SAMAN portable toilet cabin starts at ₹90,000 for the standard 8 × 8 ft steel unit. That portable toilet cabin price covers the structural cabin, fitted pan, water inlet, ventilation, lockable door, and exterior finish. What moves the price up: a larger or two-cubicle layout, a septic-tank build, a specific pan type, or a custom exterior colour.

A portable toilet cabin delivered and off-loaded onto its pad — installed in a single day on an accessible site.

Delivery is from SAMAN's own facilities, and a standard unit is off-loaded, levelled, and connected in a single day on an accessible site.

Choosing Between a Full Cabin, a Cabin-With-Toilet, and a Movable Unit

Three nearby products get confused, so here is the plain difference. This page is the full standalone cabin — the toilet is the whole structure, fixed in one position. If instead you need a room you work or rest in that happens to include a bathroom, that is a cabin with a built-in toilet — a different product where the toilet is one section of a larger living or office cabin. And if you need a sanitation unit that gets repositioned as work moves along a road or pipeline, look at the relocatable toilet format, which is built for shifting rather than staying put.

Water inlet and septic-tank connection on a portable toilet cabin — set up for sites without a mains line.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is a toilet cabin called?

It is most often called a portable toilet cabin or simply a portable toilet. Some buyers also call it a portable sanitation unit or, when it is a heavier steel build, an MS portable toilet cabin. They all describe the same thing: a standalone enclosed toilet structure.

What is the price of a portable toilet cabin in India?

A SAMAN portable toilet cabin starts at ₹90,000 for the standard 8 × 8 ft steel unit. The final price depends on size, the number of cubicles, whether a septic tank is built in, and the pan type you choose. Share your requirement for an exact quote.

Is a portable toilet cabin the same as a portable cabin with a toilet?

No. A portable toilet cabin is a standalone unit where the toilet is the entire product. A portable cabin with a toilet is a larger living or office cabin that has a bathroom inside it as one room. Choose by whether you need only sanitation or a whole room.

Does a portable toilet cabin need a water connection?

Not always. It can run off a mains water inlet, an overhead tank filled by your team, or a septic/bio arrangement that needs no mains line at all. You specify the water provision when you order, based on what your site has.

Get a Quote

Tell us your site location, the size you need, and your water arrangement, and SAMAN will send a portable toilet cabin quote with delivery timing. Call +91 9708989937 or send your requirement on WhatsApp for a same-day response.
